columns.extraction_plan: In the overall population, the addition of abiraterone to SOC (with or without docetaxel and with or without radiotherapy) decreased the number of radiographic progression events or deaths from 371 to 252, improved the median progression-free survival from 2.22 years (IQR 1.09–6.03) to 4.46 years (1.72-not reached), and reduced the relative risk of radiographic progression or death by 46% compared with patients who did not receive abiraterone (adjusted HR for radiographic progression-free survival 0.54, 99.9% CI 0.41-0.71; p<0.0001; figure 2A, table 2). Extract: "46% reduction in relative risk of radiographic progression or death". Note: This is a relative risk reduction, not a direct PFS rate. The median PFS is also reported as 4.46 years (1.72-not reached).

In the overall population, the addition of abiraterone to SOC (with or without docetaxel and with or without radiotherapy) decreased the number of radiographic progression events or deaths from 371 to 252, improved the median progression-free survival from 2.22 years (IQR 1.09–6.03) to 4.46 years (1.72-not reached), and reduced the relative risk of radiographic progression or death by 46% compared with patients who did not receive abiraterone (adjusted HR for radiographic progression-free survival 0.54, 99.9% CI 0.41-0.71; p<0.0001; figure 2A, table 2). Extract: "median progression-free survival from 2.22 years (IQR 1.09–6.03)". Note: This is the median PFS, not a direct PFS rate.
